tasks.title: Add Web UI for export schedules and history
evidence: Opportunity type: integration / feature gap
Export schedules are fully implemented in API and CLI (internal/api/export_schedules.go, internal/cli/manage/export_schedule.go) including history and cloud destinations.
Web UI has no export schedule component and App does not render one (web/src/App.tsx; no export schedule components in web/src/components).
plan: Create an ExportScheduleManager component to list, create, enable/disable, delete schedules and view history using /v1/export-schedules endpoints.
Wire the new manager into web/src/App.tsx with a clear section anchor and navigation affordance.
Add form validation and error handling for destination-specific fields (cloud/local/webhook).
Add component tests for CRUD + history view using mocked API responses.
Update docs/usage.md to include export schedule UI workflow and CLI parity notes.
Verify by running `cd web && pnpm run test` and manually checking the UI via `make web-dev` for list/create/history flows.
notes: Why it matters: scheduled exports eliminate manual runs and enable continuous delivery to S3/GCS/Azure/webhooks.
Proposed approach: MVS delivers list/create/history with local and webhook destinations; expand to cloud destination testing, presets, and bulk enable/disable.
Risks/mitigations: config complexity and secret handling; mitigate with validation, inline help, and no secret echo in UI.
Acceptance criteria: UI CRUD + history works; API errors surfaced; tests cover form validation and history rendering; docs updated.

tasks.title: Surface feed monitoring in the Web UI
evidence: Opportunity type: feature gap / UX improvement
Feed monitoring is implemented in API and CLI (internal/api/feed.go, internal/cli/manage/feed.go).
FeedManager component exists but is not mounted anywhere in the UI (web/src/components/FeedManager.tsx; no App.tsx usage).
plan: Add a FeedContainer or integrate FeedManager into App with API calls for list/create/update/delete/check/items.
Expose feed monitoring as a dedicated section with empty state and actions.
Add UI hooks for manual checks and feed item viewing with error feedback.
Add tests for create/update/check flows with mocked API responses.
Document feed monitoring in docs/usage.md (CLI + Web UI parity).
Verify by running `cd web && pnpm run test` and creating/checking a feed via `make web-dev`.
notes: Why it matters: RSS/Atom monitoring is a core workflow for ongoing research and alerts; UI access reduces friction vs CLI-only.
Proposed approach: MVS lists feeds, creates/edits, checks now, and shows seen items; expand to notifications and linking items to scrape jobs.
Risks/mitigations: scheduler concurrency limits; mitigate with clear status and rate hints.
Acceptance criteria: UI can CRUD feeds, run checks, and display items; tests cover happy/failure paths; docs updated.

tasks.title: Add webhook delivery dashboard for debugging
evidence: Opportunity type: reliability / UX improvement
Webhook delivery history endpoints exist (internal/api/webhook_handlers.go, /v1/webhooks/deliveries).
Web UI does not expose webhook delivery records or details (no components; App.tsx has no webhook section).
plan: Create a WebhookDeliveries component to list deliveries with filters (job ID, status) and drill-down detail.
Wire it into App.tsx and add navigation anchor.
Add client-side pagination and display retry counts/latency.
Add tests for list and detail rendering with mocked API responses.
Update docs/usage.md with webhook delivery troubleshooting section.
Verify by running `cd web && pnpm run test` and viewing deliveries via `make web-dev` with sample data.
notes: Why it matters: failed webhooks are a common integration failure; delivery visibility reduces debugging time and missed downstream processing.
Proposed approach: MVS read-only list + detail view; expand to retry/resend and alerting hooks.
Risks/mitigations: large delivery history; mitigate with pagination and filters.
Acceptance criteria: UI shows deliveries and detail payload; errors surfaced; tests cover filters; docs updated.

tasks.title: Expose retention status and cleanup controls in Web UI
evidence: Opportunity type: cost / reliability
Retention engine + CLI exist (internal/retention/retention.go, internal/cli/manage/retention.go) with status + cleanup options.
docs/usage.md and Web UI do not mention or expose retention controls (no retention section in App.tsx).
plan: Add a RetentionStatus panel that calls the retention status endpoint and displays storage/job stats.
Add a cleanup action with dry-run default and clear warnings.
Surface retention config values and link to .env settings.
Add tests covering dry-run and cleanup action flows with mocked responses.
Update docs/usage.md with retention section and safety notes.
Verify by running `cd web && pnpm run test` and manually triggering dry-run cleanup in `make web-dev`.
notes: Why it matters: retention controls prevent disk exhaustion and keep long-running instances stable.
Proposed approach: MVS read-only status + dry-run cleanup; expand to policy editing and scheduled cleanup review.
Risks/mitigations: accidental deletion; mitigate with dry-run default and explicit confirmations.
Acceptance criteria: status panel displays key metrics; cleanup action works; tests cover confirmation path; docs updated.

tasks.title: Make Dedup Explorer accessible in the Web UI
evidence: Opportunity type: UX improvement / reliability
Dedup endpoints exist (internal/api/dedup.go) and a UI component is already implemented (web/src/components/DedupExplorer.tsx).
The Dedup Explorer is not mounted in App.tsx, making the capability effectively hidden.
plan: Add DedupExplorer section to App.tsx with API base URL passed in.
Add navigation anchor and brief guidance text for simhash/threshold usage.
Wire optional delete-by-job action if needed for cleanup.
Add tests that validate search/history/stats rendering with mocked API responses.
Document dedup UI usage in docs/usage.md.
Verify by running `cd web && pnpm run test` and exercising the UI in `make web-dev`.
notes: Why it matters: duplicate detection improves crawl efficiency and prevents wasted storage/compute.
Proposed approach: MVS makes the existing explorer visible; expand to job-level cleanup and export of duplicate reports.
Risks/mitigations: large datasets; mitigate with pagination and cautionary messaging.
Acceptance criteria: explorer is reachable, searches work, stats render; tests cover errors; docs updated.

tasks.title: Expose template performance + A/B testing UI
evidence: Opportunity type: reliability / UX improvement
Template metrics endpoints exist (internal/api/template_metrics_handler.go) and UI components are implemented (TemplatePerformance, TemplateABTestManager).
These components are not referenced in App.tsx, so the capability is hidden from users.
plan: Add a Templates Insights section that renders TemplatePerformance and TemplateABTestManager.
Wire data loading to /v1/template-metrics and comparison endpoints with error handling.
Add a lightweight empty state when no metrics are available.
Add tests for metrics rendering and AB test creation flows.
Update docs/usage.md with template performance and A/B testing guidance.
Verify by running `cd web && pnpm run test` and reviewing the section in `make web-dev`.
notes: Why it matters: measuring template accuracy/success rates improves extraction quality and reduces regressions.
Proposed approach: MVS exposes existing dashboards; expand to experiment tagging and automated recommendations.
Risks/mitigations: small sample sizes; mitigate with warnings and minimum sample thresholds.
Acceptance criteria: UI renders metrics and A/B results; tests cover empty + populated states; docs updated.

tasks.title: Add AI template generator to the templates UI
evidence: Opportunity type: feature gap
AI template generation endpoint exists (internal/api/ai_extract.go, POST /v1/extract/ai-template-generate) and is documented in docs/usage.md.
No web UI surface calls aiTemplateGenerate (search in web/src shows no usage).
plan: Add a Generate-with-AI modal in the templates section accepting URL/HTML + prompt/fields.
Call aiTemplateGenerate, preview the generated template, and allow save to templates store.
Add error handling for missing AI config and API failures.
Add tests for successful generation and failure states.
Update docs/usage.md with UI steps and cost/limit notes.
Verify by running `cd web && pnpm run test` and using `make web-dev` to generate a template.
notes: Why it matters: AI-assisted template creation removes manual selector work and speeds onboarding.
Proposed approach: MVS adds UI flow with preview/save; expand to iterative refinement and diff against existing templates.
Risks/mitigations: API costs and prompt quality; mitigate with token limits and guidance.
Acceptance criteria: UI can generate and save templates; errors surfaced; tests cover modal flow; docs updated.

tasks.title: Create UI + CLI editors for render profiles and pipeline JS
evidence: Opportunity type: UX improvement / integration
Render profiles and pipeline JS are configured via JSON files and CLI only supports list (internal/cli/manage/render_profiles.go, internal/cli/manage/pipeline_js_mgmt.go).
docs/usage.md instructs manual edits to DATA_DIR/render_profiles.json and pipeline_js.json; Web UI lacks editors.
plan: Add CRUD CLI subcommands for render-profiles and pipeline-js (add/update/delete) with validation.
Build Web UI editors with form validation and JSON preview for each config type.
Wire editors into App.tsx with clear sections and empty-state guidance.
Add tests for CLI parsing/validation and UI form validation.
Update docs/usage.md with new commands and UI workflows.
Verify by running `make test-ci` and manually creating a profile in `make web-dev`.
notes: Why it matters: render profiles and per-target JS are core to scraping reliability; editing JSON by hand is error-prone.
Proposed approach: MVS provides form-based editors + CLI CRUD; expand to per-host testing and profile import/export.
Risks/mitigations: invalid config breaks scraping; mitigate with schema validation and dry-run tests.
Acceptance criteria: CRUD works via CLI + UI; validation blocks bad configs; tests cover both; docs updated.

tasks.title: Add OAuth2 CLI support for auth profiles
evidence: Opportunity type: integration / feature gap
OAuth endpoints are implemented in API (internal/api/auth.go) and Web UI exposes OAuth config (web/src/components/AuthConfig.tsx).
CLI has no OAuth subcommands (rg internal/cli shows no oauth references).
plan: Add `spartan auth oauth` subcommands: discover, initiate, refresh, revoke, and token-status.
For initiate, print authorization URL and instructions; optional local callback helper for token capture.
Store/retrieve tokens via existing OAuth store in internal/auth.
Add CLI tests covering expected outputs and error paths.
Update docs/usage.md with OAuth CLI examples and security notes.
Verify by running `go test ./internal/cli/...` and a manual OAuth flow against a test provider.
notes: Why it matters: OAuth-protected targets are common; CLI parity enables automation and headless use.
Proposed approach: MVS adds non-interactive initiate/refresh/revoke; expand to local callback capture and PKCE helpers.
Risks/mitigations: secrets exposure; mitigate by redacting tokens in logs and requiring explicit output flags.
Acceptance criteria: OAuth CLI flows work; tests cover failures; docs updated.

tasks.title: Refresh docs for hidden CLI + API surfaces (watch/feed/batch/chains/retention/GraphQL/WS)
evidence: Opportunity type: UX improvement
docs/usage.md lists primary CLI commands but omits detailed sections for watch, feed, batch, chains, retention, export-schedule, and GraphQL/WS endpoints.
CLI help includes these subcommands (internal/cli/help.go) and API exposes /graphql and /v1/ws (internal/api/server.go).
plan: Audit CLI help and OpenAPI for missing docs sections.
Add concise usage sections for watch/feed/batch/chains/retention/export-schedule.
Document GraphQL and WebSocket endpoints with minimal examples.
Add a short capability matrix linking CLI/API/Web UI features.
Verify by running `rg 'watch|feed|batch|chains|retention' docs/usage.md` and ensuring coverage.
notes: Why it matters: hidden capabilities reduce adoption and create support churn; docs are the fastest path to unlock value.
Proposed approach: MVS adds missing sections and examples; expand to dedicated docs pages per feature.
Risks/mitigations: doc drift; mitigate with references to CLI help and OpenAPI as source of truth.
Acceptance criteria: docs cover all listed capabilities with examples; no contradictions with CLI help.
